title: Suspected Heart Attack
symptoms: Pain radiating to the jaw, neck, back, or arms (especially the left arm)
immediate_steps: Have the person sit down, rest, and try to keep calm (W position or half-sitting is often best for breathing)
Ask if they take chest pain medication (e.g., nitroglycerin) and assist them in taking it
Offer them a standard adult aspirin (325mg) to chew and swallow, if they are not allergic and have no bleeding disorders
Be ready to perform CPR if they become unresponsive
avoid: Do not let the person drive themselves to the hospital
Do not ignore the symptoms, even if they seem mild
Do not let the person exert themselves
Do not give aspirin if they are allergic or have been told not to take it by a doctor
